DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.'S MOTION TO DEEM ADMISSIONS AND OF NONAPPEARANCE. THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF'S UNOPPOSED MOTION TO DEEM RFAS ADMITTED IS GRANTED. UNDER THE CIVIL DISCOVERY ACT, "THE COURT IS REQUIRED TO GRANT A DEEMED ADMISSION MOTION" IF THE RESPONDENT FAILS TO SERVE VERIFIED RESPONSES "BEFORE THE HEARING ON THAT MOTION." (TOBIN V. ORIS (1992) 3 CAL.APP.4TH 814, 828, DISAPPROVED ON OTHER GROUNDS OF BY WILCOX V. BIRTWHISTLE (1999) 21 CAL.4TH 973.) DEFENDANT FAILED TO RESPOND TO THE RFAS. PLAINTIFF TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS VERBATIM WITH THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312 DIRECTLY BY EMAIL TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM AT MPRITT@BSFLLP.COM. JUDGE PRO TEM: MAXELL PRITT, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Sep-20-2019 Vacated

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-07-2019 Set For Trial On Oct-15-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Deem Admissions And Of Nonappearance
Off Calendar Apr-18-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Tomas Martinez, 415-699-4653 Defendant in Pro Per.
Spanish Interpreter Jean Garascia assists Defendant.
No other Appearance.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court informs the Defendant he has not received any contest email and explains the basis for the tentative ruling. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.'s Motion To Deem Admissions And Of Nonappearance.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Plaintiff's unopposed motion to deem RFAs admitted is granted. Under the Civil Discovery Act, "the court is required to grant a deemed admission motion" if the respondent fails to serve verified responses "before the hearing on that motion." (Tobin v. Oris (1992) 3 Cal.App.4th 814, 828, disapproved on other grounds of by Wilcox v. Birtwhistle (1999) 21 Cal.4th 973.) Defendant failed to respond to the RFAs.

Plaintiff to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312 directly by email to the Judge Pro Tem at mpritt@bsfllp.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Maxell Pritt, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
